trajectory_summary: The call was lost before Bucky deployed a single substantive angle. Daniel arrived with a closed format preference, a prior sales background, and a hard time constraint — three compounding factors Bucky never addressed individually or together. Energy leaked steadily from the opening redirect through the final policy statement, with no moment where Bucky gained ground.
outcome_dispute: The v1 outcome tag is listed as 'unknown' — the observable outcome is a clean no-book with Daniel explicitly requesting removal from the list. There is no tentative appointment, no callback scheduled, and no bill collected. The outcome should be tagged as lost_lead / no_appointment.

primary_coaching_focus.topic: Frame discipline under pressure — don't narrate the script
primary_coaching_focus.quote: I'm supposed to sound really cocky. I'm not gonna lie.
primary_coaching_focus.why: Bucky broke frame aloud to a customer who had already identified himself as a former solar salesperson. This single line handed Daniel confirmation that he was talking to a scripted setter, not a peer — and it came at the exact moment Bucky needed credibility most. The coaching priority is not tone or confidence in the abstract; it is the specific reflex of narrating script discomfort under pressure. This rep needs to understand what that line cost him before any other technique is layered on.

key_moments.quote: I'm supposed to sound really cocky. I'm not gonna lie.
key_moments.stewart_read: This is the highest-leverage missed moment in the call. Bucky breaks frame by narrating his own script anxiety aloud — to a customer who has already identified himself as a former solar salesperson. The schema's trust_building_micro_trades directive looks for moments where a rep substitutes a personal commitment for an impersonal process. Here Bucky does the opposite: he exposes the process seam at exactly the moment Daniel needed to see a peer, not a script. Strategic hypothesis: Bucky was attempting self-deprecating rapport to disarm Daniel. Reactive hypothesis: Bucky lost composure under pressure and said the quiet part loud. The reactive read holds — there is no subsequent move that capitalizes on the disclosure, and Daniel's response ('that's what everybody says') confirms it landed as confirmation of his suspicion, not as rapport.

key_moments.quote: I don't wanna waste anybody's time. That's why I said via email.
key_moments.stewart_read: Daniel's 'I don't wanna waste anybody's time' is a guilt-wrapped soft refusal — the schema directive names this pattern explicitly. He is framing his email preference as a favor to Bucky, which hands Bucky a reframe opportunity: if Daniel controls the format, he controls whether time is wasted. Bucky does not take the reframe; he pivots to enthusiasm ('we're one of the best') which Daniel neutralizes immediately with his sales background. Strategic hypothesis: Bucky was setting up a later credibility play. Reactive hypothesis: Bucky missed the reframe and defaulted to enthusiasm. The reactive read holds — the enthusiasm play lands flat and no reframe is attempted.

key_moments.quote: what if I cut the in person appointment and I all that sit down stuff? If I remove that and I set you up with just a phone call and all they do is they call you, they Gmail you, and then you guys just go over it together
key_moments.stewart_read: Bucky makes a format concession — dropping the in-person framing for a phone call — without extracting any commitment in return. This is a negotiation discipline gap: Bucky gave something (format flexibility) and got nothing back (not even a conditional 'if it's a phone call, I'll do it'). The schema's multi_angle_resilience section is TBD, so full scoring against that standard is not yet possible. What is observable: this was Bucky's first substantive angle, and it was a concession rather than a reframe. Strategic hypothesis: Bucky was lowering the barrier to get Daniel to agree to any format, planning to lock the commitment afterward. Reactive hypothesis: Bucky accommodated Daniel's preference without a plan for what came next. The reactive read holds — Daniel's response ('I want that just in an email') shows the concession did not move him; it only confirmed that further concessions were available.

key_moments.quote: I'm actually supposed to be joining a meeting in less than five minutes, but I gotta get on.
key_moments.stewart_read: Daniel introduces a hard time constraint at 03:38. The schema's time_constraint protocol is explicit: anchor the time confidently ('it's a 30-minute call — we'll get you in and out'). Bucky never deploys this. The protocol was available and unused. This is not a strategic omission — by 03:38 the call is already in exit mode, and Bucky's response ('I'm gonna try one more time just because I have to') confirms he is not reading the time constraint as a protocol trigger. The missed application is earlier: Daniel's 'I don't have much time' signal at 03:43 was the moment to anchor.
